mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving factory utilization and output growth, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production signals fail to predict positive 5-day returns during easing periods with accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes exceeding 0.4% month-over-month are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, driving positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts alongside stagnant personal spending signals weakening demand in interest-sensitive sectors, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter during late-cycle phases.
failure_condition: Housing and spending declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns in late-cycle regimes with accuracy below 50%.
